NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Contemplation of the sons of Korah. We have heard with our ears, O God, Our fathers have told us, The deeds You did in their days, In days of old:
NKJV.2: You drove out the nations with Your hand, But them You planted; You afflicted the peoples, and cast them out.
NKJV.3: For they did not gain possession of the land by their own sword, Nor did their own arm save them; But it was Your right hand, Your arm, and the light of Your countenance,  Because You favored them.
NKJV.4: You are my King, O God; Command victories for Jacob.
NKJV.5: Through You we will push down our enemies; Through Your name we will trample those who rise up against us.
NKJV.6: For I will not trust in my bow, Nor shall my sword save me.
NKJV.7: But You have saved us from our enemies, And have put to shame those who hated us.
NKJV.8: In God we boast all day long, And praise Your name forever. Selah
NKJV.9: But You have cast us off and put us to shame, And You do not go out with our armies.
NKJV.10: You make us turn back from the enemy, And those who hate us have taken spoil for themselves.
NKJV.11: You have given us up like sheep intended for food, And have scattered us among the nations.
NKJV.12: You sell Your people for next to nothing, And are not enriched by selling them.
NKJV.13: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, A scorn and a derision to those all around us.
NKJV.14: You make us a byword among the nations,  A shaking of the head among the peoples.
NKJV.15: My dishonor is continually before me, And the shame of my face has covered me,
NKJV.16: Because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles,  Because of the enemy and the avenger.
NKJV.17: All this has come upon us; But we have not forgotten You, Nor have we dealt falsely with Your covenant.
NKJV.18: Our heart has not turned back,  Nor have our steps departed from Your way;
NKJV.19: But You have severely broken us in the place of jackals, And covered us with the shadow of death.
NKJV.20: If we had forgotten the name of our God, Or stretched out our hands to a foreign god,
NKJV.21: Would not God search this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
NKJV.22: Yet for Your sake we are killed all day long; We are accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
NKJV.23: Awake! Why do You sleep, O Lord? Arise! Do not cast us off forever.
NKJV.24: Why do You hide Your face, And forget our affliction and our oppression?
NKJV.25: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; Our body clings to the ground.
NKJV.26: Arise for our help, And redeem us for Your mercies’ sake.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> for the sons<S>1121</S> of Korah<S>7141</S>, Maschil<S>4905</S>. We have heard<S>8085</S> with our ears<S>241</S>, O God<S>430</S>, our fathers<S>1</S> have told<S>5608</S> us, what work<S>6467</S> thou didst<S>6466</S> in their days<S>3117</S>, in the times<S>3117</S> of old<S>6924</S>.

KJV.7: But thou hast saved<S>3467</S> us from our enemies<S>6862</S>, and hast put them to shame<S>954</S> that hated<S>8130</S> us.
KJV.8: In God<S>430</S> we boast<S>1984</S> all the day<S>3117</S> long, and praise<S>3034</S> thy name<S>8034</S> for ever<S>5769</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.9: But<S>637</S> thou hast cast off<S>2186</S>, and put us to shame<S>3637</S>; and goest not forth<S>3318</S> with our armies<S>6635</S>.
KJV.10: Thou makest us to turn<S>7725</S> back<S>268</S> from the enemy<S>6862</S>: and they which hate<S>8130</S> us spoil<S>8154</S> for themselves.
KJV.11: Thou hast given<S>5414</S> us like sheep<S>6629</S> appointed for meat<S>3978</S>; and hast scattered<S>2219</S> us among the heathen<S>1471</S>. <sup>like: Heb. as sheep of meat</sup>

KJV.13: Thou makest<S>7760</S> us a reproach<S>2781</S> to our neighbours<S>7934</S>, a scorn<S>3933</S> and a derision<S>7047</S> to them that are round about<S>5439</S> us.
KJV.14: Thou makest<S>7760</S> us a byword<S>4912</S> among the heathen<S>1471</S>, a shaking<S>4493</S> of the head<S>7218</S> among the people<S>3816</S>.
KJV.15: My confusion<S>3639</S> is continually<S>3117</S> before me, and the shame<S>1322</S> of my face<S>6440</S> hath covered<S>3680</S> me,
KJV.16: For the voice<S>6963</S> of him that reproacheth<S>2778</S> and blasphemeth<S>1442</S>; by reason<S>6440</S> of the enemy<S>341</S> and avenger<S>5358</S>.

KJV.21: Shall not God<S>430</S> search this out<S>2713</S>? for he knoweth<S>3045</S> the secrets<S>8587</S> of the heart<S>3820</S>.

KJV.26: Arise<S>6965</S> for our help<S>5833</S>, and redeem<S>6299</S> us for thy mercies'<S>2617</S> sake. <sup>for our: Heb. a help for us</sup>

ESV.1: O God, we have heard with our ears, our fathers have told us, what deeds you performed in their days, in the days of old:
ESV.2: you with your own hand drove out the nations, but them you planted; you afflicted the peoples, but them you set free;
ESV.3: for not by their own sword did they win the land, nor did their own arm save them, but your right hand and your arm, and the light of your face, for you delighted in them.
ESV.4: You are my King, O God; ordain salvation for Jacob!
ESV.5: Through you we push down our foes; through your name we tread down those who rise up against us.
ESV.6: For not in my bow do I trust, nor can my sword save me.
ESV.7: But you have saved us from our foes and have put to shame those who hate us.
ESV.8: In God we have boasted continually, and we will give thanks to your name forever. Selah
ESV.9: But you have rejected us and disgraced us and have not gone out with our armies.
ESV.10: You have made us turn back from the foe, and those who hate us have gotten spoil.
ESV.11: You have made us like sheep for slaughter and have scattered us among the nations.
ESV.12: You have sold your people for a trifle, demanding no high price for them.
ESV.13: You have made us the taunt of our neighbors, the derision and scorn of those around us.
ESV.14: You have made us a byword among the nations, a laughingstock  among the peoples.
ESV.15: All day long my disgrace is before me, and shame has covered my face
ESV.16: at the sound of the taunter and reviler, at the sight of the enemy and the avenger.
ESV.17: All this has come upon us, though we have not forgotten you, and we have not been false to your covenant.
ESV.18: Our heart has not turned back, nor have our steps departed from your way;
ESV.19: yet you have broken us in the place of jackals and covered us with the shadow of death.
ESV.20: If we had forgotten the name of our God or spread out our hands to a foreign god,
ESV.21: would not God discover this? For he knows the secrets of the heart.
ESV.22: Yet for your sake we are killed all the day long; we are regarded as sheep to be slaughtered.
ESV.23: Awake! Why are you sleeping, O Lord? Rouse yourself! Do not reject us forever!
ESV.24: Why do you hide your face? Why do you forget our affliction and oppression?
ESV.25: For our soul is bowed down to the dust; our belly clings to the ground.
ESV.26: Rise up; come to our help! Redeem us for the sake of your steadfast love!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Maskil of the sons of Korah. O God, we have heard with our ears, Our fathers have told us The work that You did in their days, In the days of old.
NASB.2: You with Your own hand drove out the nations; Then You planted them; You afflicted the peoples, Then You spread them abroad.
NASB.3: For by their own sword they did not possess the land, And their own arm did not save them, But Your right hand and Your arm and the light of Your presence, For You favored them.
NASB.4: You are my King, O God; Command victories for Jacob.
NASB.5: Through You we will push back our adversaries; Through Your name we will trample down those who rise up against us.
NASB.6: For I will not trust in my bow, Nor will my sword save me.
NASB.7: But You have saved us from our adversaries, And You have put to shame those who hate us.
NASB.8: In God we have boasted all day long, And we will give thanks to Your name forever. Selah.
NASB.9: Yet You have rejected [us] and brought us to dishonor, And do not go out with our armies.
NASB.10: You cause us to turn back from the adversary; And those who hate us have taken spoil for themselves.
NASB.11: You give us as sheep to be eaten And have scattered us among the nations.
NASB.12: You sell Your people cheaply, And have not profited by their sale.
NASB.13: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, A scoffing and a derision to those around us.
NASB.14: You make us a byword among the nations, A laughingstock among the peoples.
NASB.15: All day long my dishonor is before me And my humiliation has overwhelmed me,
NASB.16: Because of the voice of him who reproaches and reviles, Because of the presence of the enemy and the avenger.
NASB.17: All this has come upon us, but we have not forgotten You, And we have not dealt falsely with Your covenant.
NASB.18: Our heart has not turned back, And our steps have not deviated from Your way,
NASB.19: Yet You have crushed us in a place of jackals And covered us with the shadow of death.
NASB.20: If we had forgotten the name of our God Or extended our hands to a strange god,
NASB.21: Would not God find this out? For He knows the secrets of the heart.
NASB.22: But for Your sake we are killed all day long; We are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.
NASB.23: Arouse Yourself, why do You sleep, O Lord? Awake, do not reject us forever.
NASB.24: Why do You hide Your face [And] forget our affliction and our oppression?
NASB.25: For our soul has sunk down into the dust; Our body cleaves to the earth.
NASB.26: Rise up, be our help, And redeem us for the sake of Your lovingkindness.

NLT.1:
O God, we have heard it with our own ears —
our ancestors have told us
of all you did in their day,
in days long ago:
NLT.2:
You drove out the pagan nations by your power
and gave all the land to our ancestors.
You crushed their enemies
and set our ancestors free.
NLT.3:
They did not conquer the land with their swords;
it was not their own strong arm that gave them victory.
It was your right hand and strong arm
and the blinding light from your face that helped them,
for you loved them.
NLT.4:
You are my King and my God.
You command victories for Israel.
NLT.5:
Only by your power can we push back our enemies;
only in your name can we trample our foes.
NLT.6:
I do not trust in my bow;
I do not count on my sword to save me.
NLT.7:
You are the one who gives us victory over our enemies;
you disgrace those who hate us.
NLT.8:
O God, we give glory to you all day long
and constantly praise your name. Interlude
NLT.9:
But now you have tossed us aside in dishonor.
You no longer lead our armies to battle.
NLT.10:
You make us retreat from our enemies
and allow those who hate us to plunder our land.
NLT.11:
You have butchered us like sheep
and scattered us among the nations.
NLT.12:
You sold your precious people for a pittance,
making nothing on the sale.
NLT.13:
You let our neighbors mock us.
We are an object of scorn and derision to those around us.
NLT.14:
You have made us the butt of their jokes;
they shake their heads at us in scorn.
NLT.15:
We can’t escape the constant humiliation;
shame is written across our faces.
NLT.16:
All we hear are the taunts of our mockers.
All we see are our vengeful enemies.
NLT.17:
All this has happened though we have not forgotten you.
We have not violated your covenant.
NLT.18:
Our hearts have not deserted you.
We have not strayed from your path.
NLT.19:
Yet you have crushed us in the jackal’s desert home.
You have covered us with darkness and death.
NLT.20:
If we had forgotten the name of our God
or spread our hands in prayer to foreign gods,
NLT.21:
God would surely have known it,
for he knows the secrets of every heart.
NLT.22:
But for your sake we are killed every day;
we are being slaughtered like sheep.
NLT.23:
Wake up, O Lord! Why do you sleep?
Get up! Do not reject us forever.
NLT.24:
Why do you look the other way?
Why do you ignore our suffering and oppression?
NLT.25:
We collapse in the dust,
lying face down in the dirt.
NLT.26:
Rise up! Help us!
Ransom us because of your unfailing love.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. By the sons of Korah. A contemplative psalm. We have heard with our ears, God; our fathers have told us, what work you did in their days, in the days of old.
WEB.2: You drove out the nations with your hand, but you planted them. You afflicted the peoples, but you spread them abroad.
WEB.3: For they didn’t get the land in possession by their own sword, neither did their own arm save them; but your right hand, and your arm, and the light of your face, because you were favorable to them.
WEB.4: You are my King, God. Command victories for Jacob!
WEB.5: Through you, will we push down our adversaries. Through your name, will we tread them under who rise up against us.
WEB.6: For I will not trust in my bow, neither shall my sword save me.
WEB.7: But you have saved us from our adversaries, and have shamed those who hate us.
WEB.8: In God we have made our boast all day long, we will give thanks to your name forever. Selah.
WEB.9: But now you rejected us, and brought us to dishonor, and don’t go out with our armies.
WEB.10: You make us turn back from the adversary. Those who hate us take plunder for themselves.
WEB.11: You have made us like sheep for food, and have scattered us among the nations.
WEB.12: You sell your people for nothing, and have gained nothing from their sale.
WEB.13: You make us a reproach to our neighbors, a scoffing and a derision to those who are around us.
WEB.14: You make us a byword among the nations, a shaking of the head among the peoples.
WEB.15: All day long my dishonor is before me, and shame covers my face,
WEB.16: At the taunt of one who reproaches and verbally abuses, because of the enemy and the avenger.
WEB.17: All this has come on us, yet have we not forgotten you, Neither have we been false to your covenant.
WEB.18: Our heart has not turned back, neither have our steps strayed from your path,
WEB.19: Though you have crushed us in the haunt of jackals, and covered us with the shadow of death.
WEB.20: If we have forgotten the name of our God, or spread out our hands to a strange god;
WEB.21: won’t God search this out? For he knows the secrets of the heart.
WEB.22: Yes, for your sake we are killed all day long. We are regarded as sheep for the slaughter.
WEB.23: Wake up! Why do you sleep, Lord?  Arise! Don’t reject us forever.
WEB.24: Why do you hide your face, and forget our affliction and our oppression?
WEB.25: For our soul is bowed down to the dust. Our body clings to the earth.
WEB.26: Rise up to help us. Redeem us for your loving kindness’ sake.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director. A of the sons of Korah. [1] God, we have heard with our ears —
our ancestors have told us —
the work you accomplished in their days,
in days long ago: ⓕ
CSB17.2:
In order to plant them,
you displaced the nations by your hand;
in order to settle them,
you brought disaster on the peoples. ⓖ
CSB17.3:
For they did not take the land by their sword —
their arm did not bring them victory —
but by your right hand, your arm,
and the light of your face, ⓗ
because you were favorable toward them. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
You are my King, my God,
who ordains [1] victories for Jacob. ⓙ
CSB17.5:
Through you we drive back our foes;
through your name we trample our enemies. ⓚ
CSB17.6:
For I do not trust in my bow,
and my sword does not bring me victory. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
But you give us victory over our foes
and let those who hate us be disgraced. ⓜ
CSB17.8:
We boast in God all day long;
we will praise your name forever. ⓝ Selah
CSB17.9:
But you have rejected and humiliated us;
you do not march out with our armies. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
You make us retreat from the foe,
and those who hate us
have taken plunder for themselves. ⓑ
CSB17.11:
You hand us over to be eaten like sheep
and scatter us among the nations. ⓒ
CSB17.12:
You sell your people for nothing;
you make no profit from selling them. ⓓ
CSB17.13:
You make us an object of reproach to our neighbors,
a source of mockery and ridicule to those around us. ⓔ
CSB17.14:
You make us a joke among the nations,
a laughingstock [2] among the peoples. ⓕ
CSB17.15:
My disgrace is before me all day long,
and shame has covered my face, ⓖ
CSB17.16:
because of the taunts [3] of the scorner and reviler,
because of the enemy and avenger. ⓗ
CSB17.17:
All this has happened to us,
but we have not forgotten you
or betrayed your covenant. ⓘ
CSB17.18:
Our hearts have not turned back;
our steps have not strayed from your path. ⓙ
CSB17.19:
But you have crushed us in a haunt of jackals
and have covered us with deepest darkness. ⓚ
CSB17.20:
If we had forgotten the name of our God
and spread out our hands to a foreign god, ⓛ
CSB17.21:
wouldn’t God have found this out,
since he knows the secrets of the heart? ⓜ
CSB17.22:
Because of you we are being put to death all day long;
we are counted as sheep to be slaughtered. ⓝ
CSB17.23:
Wake up, LORD! Why are you sleeping?
Get up! Don’t reject us forever! ⓐ
CSB17.24:
Why do you hide
and forget our affliction and oppression? ⓑ
CSB17.25:
For we have sunk down to the dust;
our bodies cling to the ground. ⓒ
CSB17.26:
Rise up! Help us!
Redeem us because of your faithful love. ⓓ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of the sons of Korah. A skillful song, or a didactic or reflective poem. We have heard with our ears, O God,
Our fathers have told us
The work You did in their days,
In the days of old.
AMP.2:
You drove out the [pagan] nations with Your own hand;
Then you planted and established them (Israel);
[It was by Your power that] You uprooted the [pagan] peoples,
Then You spread them abroad.
AMP.3:
For our fathers did not possess the land [of Canaan] by their own sword,
Nor did their own arm save them,
But Your right hand and Your arm and the light of Your presence,
Because You favored and delighted in them.
AMP.4:
You are my King, O God;
Command victories and deliverance for Jacob (Israel).
AMP.5:
Through You we will gore our enemies [like a bull];
Through Your name we will trample down those who rise up against us.
AMP.6:
For I will not trust in my bow,
Nor will my sword save me.
AMP.7:
But You have saved us from our enemies,
And You have put them to shame and humiliated those who hate us.
AMP.8:
In God we have boasted all the day long,
And we will praise and give thanks to Your name forever. Selah.
AMP.9:
But now You have rejected us and brought us to dishonor,
And You do not go out with our armies [to lead us to victory].
AMP.10:
You make us turn back from the enemy,
And those who hate us have taken spoil for themselves.
AMP.11:
You have made us like sheep to be eaten [as mutton]
And have scattered us [in exile] among the nations.
AMP.12:
You sell Your people cheaply,
And have not increased Your wealth by their sale.
AMP.13:
You have made us the reproach and taunt of our neighbors,
A scoffing and a derision to those around us.
AMP.14:
You make us a byword among the nations,
A laughingstock among the people.
AMP.15:
My dishonor is before me all day long,
And humiliation has covered my face,
AMP.16:
Because of the voice of the taunter and reviler,
Because of the presence of the enemy and the avenger.
AMP.17:
All this has come upon us, yet we have not forgotten You,
Nor have we been false to Your covenant [which You made with our fathers].
AMP.18:
Our heart has not turned back,
Nor have our steps wandered from Your path,
AMP.19:
Yet You have [distressingly] crushed us in the place of jackals
And covered us with [the deep darkness of] the shadow of death.
AMP.20:
If we had forgotten the name of our God
Or stretched out our hands to a strange god,
AMP.21:
Would not God discover this?
For He knows the secrets of the heart.
AMP.22:
But for Your sake we are killed all the day long;
We are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.
AMP.23:
Awake! Why do You sleep, O Lord?
Awaken, do not reject us forever.
AMP.24:
Why do You hide Your face
And forget our affliction and our oppression?
AMP.25:
For our life has melted away into the dust;
Our body clings to the ground.
AMP.26:
Rise up! Come be our help,
And ransom us for the sake of Your steadfast love.

MSG.1:
A psalm of the sons of Korah
We've been hearing about this, God,
all our lives.
Our fathers told us the stories
their fathers told them,
MSG.2:
How single-handedly you weeded out the godless
from the fields and planted us,
How you sent those people packing
but gave us a fresh start.
MSG.3:
We didn't fight for this land;
we didn't work for it—it was a gift!
You gave it, smiling as you gave it,
delighting as you gave it.
MSG.4:
You're my King, O God—
command victories for Jacob!
MSG.5:
With your help we'll wipe out our enemies,
in your name we'll stomp them to dust.
MSG.6:
I don't trust in weapons;
my sword won't save me—
MSG.7:
But it's you, you who saved us from the enemy;
you made those who hate us lose face.
MSG.8:
All day we parade God's praise—
we thank you by name over and over.
MSG.9:
But now you've walked off and left us,
you've disgraced us and won't fight for us.
MSG.10:
You made us turn tail and run;
those who hate us have cleaned us out.
MSG.11:
You delivered us as sheep to the butcher,
you scattered us to the four winds.
MSG.12:
You sold your people at a discount—
you made nothing on the sale.
MSG.13:
You made people on the street,
urchins, poke fun and call us names.
MSG.14:
You made us a joke among the godless,
a cheap joke among the rabble.
MSG.15:
Every day I'm up against it,
my nose rubbed in my shame—
MSG.16:
Gossip and ridicule fill the air,
people out to get me crowd the street.
MSG.17:
All this came down on us,
and we've done nothing to deserve it.
We never betrayed your Covenant:
MSG.18:
our hearts
were never false, our feet never left your path.
MSG.19:
Do we deserve torture in a den of jackals?
or lockup in a black hole?
MSG.20:
If we had forgotten to pray to our God
or made fools of ourselves with store-bought gods,
MSG.21:
Wouldn't God have figured this out?
We can't hide things from him.
MSG.22:
No, you decided to make us martyrs,
lambs assigned for sacrifice each day.
MSG.23:
Get up, God! Are you going to sleep all day?
Wake up! Don't you care what happens to us?
MSG.24:
Why do you bury your face in the pillow?
Why pretend things are just fine with us?
MSG.25:
And here we are—flat on our faces in the dirt,
held down with a boot on our necks.
MSG.26:
Get up and come to our rescue.
If you love us so much, Help us!

NIV.1:
Psalm 44
For the director of music. Of the Sons of Korah. A maskil.
We have heard with our ears, O God;
our fathers have told us
what you did in their days,
in days long ago.
NIV.2:
With your hand you drove out the nations
and planted our fathers;
you crushed the peoples
and made our fathers flourish.
NIV.3:
It was not by their sword that they won the land,
nor did their arm bring them victory;
it was your right hand, your arm,
and the light of your face, for you loved them.
NIV.4:
You are my King and my God,
who decrees victories for Jacob.
NIV.5:
Through you we push back our enemies;
through your name we trample our foes.
NIV.6:
I do not trust in my bow,
my sword does not bring me victory;
NIV.7:
but you give us victory over our enemies,
you put our adversaries to shame.
NIV.8:
In God we make our boast all day long,
and we will praise your name forever. Selah
NIV.9:
But now you have rejected and humbled us;
you no longer go out with our armies.
NIV.10:
You made us retreat before the enemy,
and our adversaries have plundered us.
NIV.11:
You gave us up to be devoured like sheep
and have scattered us among the nations.
NIV.12:
You sold your people for a pittance,
gaining nothing from their sale.
NIV.13:
You have made us a reproach to our neighbors,
the scorn and derision of those around us.
NIV.14:
You have made us a byword among the nations;
the peoples shake their heads at us.
NIV.15:
My disgrace is before me all day long,
and my face is covered with shame
NIV.16:
at the taunts of those who reproach and revile me,
because of the enemy, who is bent on revenge.
NIV.17:
All this happened to us,
though we had not forgotten you
or been false to your covenant.
NIV.18:
Our hearts had not turned back;
our feet had not strayed from your path.
NIV.19:
But you crushed us and made us a haunt for jackals
and covered us over with deep darkness.
NIV.20:
If we had forgotten the name of our God
or spread out our hands to a foreign god,
NIV.21:
would not God have discovered it,
since he knows the secrets of the heart?
NIV.22:
Yet for your sake we face death all day long;
we are considered as sheep to be slaughtered.
NIV.23:
Awake, O Lord! Why do you sleep?
Rouse yourself! Do not reject us forever.
NIV.24:
Why do you hide your face
and forget our misery and oppression?
NIV.25:
We are brought down to the dust;
our bodies cling to the ground.
NIV.26:
Rise up and help us;
redeem us because of your unfailing love.

NRSVCE.1:
We have heard with our ears, O God,
our ancestors have told us,
what deeds you performed in their days,
in the days of old:
NRSVCE.2:
you with your own hand drove out the nations,
but them you planted;
you afflicted the peoples,
but them you set free;
NRSVCE.3:
for not by their own sword did they win the land,
nor did their own arm give them victory;
but your right hand, and your arm,
and the light of your countenance,
for you delighted in them.
NRSVCE.4:
You are my King and my God;
you command victories for Jacob.
NRSVCE.5:
Through you we push down our foes;
through your name we tread down our assailants.
NRSVCE.6:
For not in my bow do I trust,
nor can my sword save me.
NRSVCE.7:
But you have saved us from our foes,
and have put to confusion those who hate us.
NRSVCE.8:
In God we have boasted continually,
and we will give thanks to your name forever. Selah
NRSVCE.9:
Yet you have rejected us and abased us,
and have not gone out with our armies.
NRSVCE.10:
You made us turn back from the foe,
and our enemies have gotten spoil.
NRSVCE.11:
You have made us like sheep for slaughter,
and have scattered us among the nations.
NRSVCE.12:
You have sold your people for a trifle,
demanding no high price for them.
NRSVCE.13:
You have made us the taunt of our neighbors,
the derision and scorn of those around us.
NRSVCE.14:
You have made us a byword among the nations,
a laughingstock among the peoples.
NRSVCE.15:
All day long my disgrace is before me,
and shame has covered my face
NRSVCE.16:
at the words of the taunters and revilers,
at the sight of the enemy and the avenger.
NRSVCE.17:
All this has come upon us,
yet we have not forgotten you,
or been false to your covenant.
NRSVCE.18:
Our heart has not turned back,
nor have our steps departed from your way,
NRSVCE.19:
yet you have broken us in the haunt of jackals,
and covered us with deep darkness.
NRSVCE.20:
If we had forgotten the name of our God,
or spread out our hands to a strange god,
NRSVCE.21:
would not God discover this?
For he knows the secrets of the heart.
NRSVCE.22:
Because of you we are being killed all day long,
and accounted as sheep for the slaughter.
NRSVCE.23:
Rouse yourself! Why do you sleep, O Lord?
Awake, do not cast us off forever!
NRSVCE.24:
Why do you hide your face?
Why do you forget our affliction and oppression?
NRSVCE.25:
For we sink down to the dust;
our bodies cling to the ground.
NRSVCE.26:
Rise up, come to our help.
Redeem us for the sake of your steadfast love.

NET.1: O God, we have clearly heard; our ancestors have told us what you did in their days, in ancient times.
NET.2: You, by your power, defeated nations and settled our fathers on their land; you crushed the people living there and enabled our ancestors to occupy it.
NET.3: For they did not conquer the land by their swords, and they did not prevail by their strength, but rather by your power, strength and good favor, for you were partial to them.
NET.4: You are my king, O God! Decree Jacob’s deliverance!
NET.5: By your power we will drive back our enemies; by your strength we will trample down our foes!
NET.6: For I do not trust in my bow, and I do not prevail by my sword.
NET.7: For you deliver us from our enemies; you humiliate those who hate us.
NET.8: In God I boast all day long, and we will continually give thanks to your name. (Selah)
NET.9: But you rejected and embarrassed us! You did not go into battle with our armies.
NET.10: You made us retreat from the enemy. Those who hate us take whatever they want from us.
NET.11: You handed us over like sheep to be eaten; you scattered us among the nations.
NET.12: You sold your people for a pittance; you did not ask a high price for them.
NET.13: You made us an object of disdain to our neighbors; those who live on our borders taunt and insult us.
NET.14: You made us an object of ridicule among the nations; foreigners treat us with contempt.
NET.15: All day long I feel humiliated and am overwhelmed with shame,
NET.16: before the vindictive enemy who ridicules and insults me.
NET.17: All this has happened to us, even though we have not rejected you or violated your covenant with us.
NET.18: We have not been unfaithful, nor have we disobeyed your commands.
NET.19: Yet you have battered us, leaving us a heap of ruins overrun by wild dogs; you have covered us with darkness.
NET.20: If we had rejected our God, and spread out our hands in prayer to another god,
NET.21: would not God discover it, for he knows one’s thoughts?
NET.22: Yet because of you we are killed all day long; we are treated like sheep at the slaughtering block.
NET.23: Rouse yourself! Why do you sleep, O Lord? Wake up! Do not reject us forever!
NET.24: Why do you look the other way, and ignore the way we are oppressed and mistreated?
NET.25: For we lie in the dirt, with our bellies pressed to the ground.
NET.26: Rise up and help us! Rescue us because of your loyal love!
